text.title: Value Stream Management Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the value-stream-management methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; the final rule defines when to skip this methodology entirely.

rule.statement: Flow analysis MUST use ≥30 completed items in the last 90 days; smaller samples yield noise, not signal.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Investment MUST target ONE bottleneck per quarter — parallel investment dilutes signal.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Flow efficiency (Process Time / Lead Time) MUST appear on every VSM report.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Lead Time MUST be reported as p50 + p85 + p95 — never as a simple mean.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Each step MUST be timestamped at entry + exit — manual log = unreliable.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Skip when sample <30 or steps not timestamped.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.
